heading: Purposes
label: 70702 Purposes
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7070236:4401 (words beginning with “for the following objects and purposes”).(words beginning with “for the following objects and purposes”), .June 6, 1900, ch. 806, § 131 Stat. 662In clause (2), the words “that are donated to, or acquired by, the corporation” are substituted for “as may be donated to said association or acquired by purchase[,] bequest, or other lawful means” to eliminate unnecessary words.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7070236:4401 (words beginning with “for the following objects and purposes”).(words beginning with “for the following objects and purposes”), .June 6, 1900, ch. 806, § 131 Stat. 662In clause (2), the words “that are donated to, or acquired by, the corporation” are substituted for “as may be donated to said association or acquired by purchase[,] bequest, or other lawful means” to eliminate unnecessary words.

children.text: to preserve to posterity the memory of the life and character of the late Frederick Douglass; and

to collect, collate, and preserve a historical record of the inception, progress, and culmination of the antislavery movement in the United States, and to assemble in the homestead of the late Frederick Douglass, commonly called Cedar Hill, in the village of Anacostia, District of Columbia, all suitable exhibits of records or things illustrative or commemorative of the antislavery movement and history that are donated to, or acquired by, the corporation.
